requirements.summary: Add responsive app shell with clear navigation between a landing page and a drone design workspace using client-side routing.
acceptanceCriteria: App has at least two routes/pages: a landing page and a design workspace.
Navigation between pages is obvious on desktop and mobile.
No placeholder lorem-ipsum remains in user-facing text.

file_operations.description: Create the root App component (imported by existing frontend/src/main.tsx) that wires client-side routing for Landing and Workspace views and renders a shared app shell (header + main content).
Define route configuration and route components for the landing page and the design workspace; ensure routes are used by frontend/src/App.tsx.
Create a responsive header with primary navigation links (Landing, Workspace) and space for auth status/logo; ensure mobile-friendly layout and obvious navigation.
Implement the landing page content (product pitch + call-to-action to open the workspace) with real English copy (no lorem ipsum).
Implement the workspace page layout (editor left, 3D preview center/right, summary + My Designs panels) with responsive stacking for mobile.

requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity sign-in/out UI, signed-in state display, and gating of save/load features when signed out; prompt for a user profile name on first login.
acceptanceCriteria: User can sign in/out with Internet Identity.
UI clearly indicates whether the user is signed in.
Saving/loading designs is disabled (with a helpful message) when signed out.

file_operations.description: Create a Login/Logout button that composes the existing immutable hook frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts and clears React Query cache on logout. Use shadcn-ui components for button styling where appropriate. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add React Query hooks that use frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts to fetch current user role and user profile (getCallerUserRole, getCallerUserProfile) and expose consolidated signed-in state for UI.

Create a modal dialog prompting for a display name the first time a principal logs in (when getCallerUserProfile returns null); save via saveCallerUserProfile. Use shadcn-ui Dialog/Form components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Show signed-in state in the header (e.g., show profile name when available, otherwise a neutral 'Signed in' label) without displaying the raw principal id. Use shadcn-ui Badge/Avatar primitives if available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire LoginButton, SignedInBadge, and ProfileSetupDialog into the shared header so auth state is visible and profile setup is triggered after successful login. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Centralize helper logic for gating persistence actions (save/load/list/rename/delete) behind authentication, returning user-friendly messages when signed out.

requirements.summary: Build a drone design editor with configurable component categories and immediate state updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace includes a component list and an editor UI to change component selections/parameters.
User can add/remove or change at least the minimum component categories listed.
The current design state updates immediately in the UI when edits are made.

file_operations.description: Define TypeScript types for the drone design configuration (frame, motors, props, battery, flight controller, camera/payload) and key adjustable parameters (quantity, KV, prop size, cell count, etc.).
Provide sensible default configuration values and option lists for the editor (common frame types/sizes, motor KV ranges, prop sizes, battery capacities/cell counts, etc.).
Create a React context/provider to hold the current design state, expose update actions, and support replacing the entire design when loading a saved design.

Implement the editor UI (component list + controls) for minimum categories: frame, motor, propeller, battery, flight controller, camera/optional payload. Use shadcn-ui Accordion/Tabs/Select/Input components for a tool-like editor. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire DesignStore provider and render the ComponentEditorPanel in the workspace layout so edits update the shared design state immediately.

requirements.summary: Render a live 3D game preview (3D game) of the configured drone using React Three Fiber with orbit controls and visual differentiation of major parts.
acceptanceCriteria: 3D preview renders in the workspace without errors.
User can rotate/zoom the view via orbit controls.
Changing components in the editor visibly changes the 3D preview (at least for frame size/type and prop size).

file_operations.description: Add/verify required dependencies for Three.js rendering: three, @react-three/fiber, and @react-three/drei (for orbit controls) so the 3D preview can be implemented.
Create a React Three Fiber Canvas wrapper with lighting and OrbitControls for rotating/zooming. Ensure it reads the current design from DesignStore.

Render a simplified parametric 3D representation of the drone with distinct materials/colors for frame, motors, props, battery, and camera/payload; map frame size/type and prop size to visible geometry changes.
Place the DronePreviewCanvas into the workspace layout and ensure it re-renders as design state changes.

requirements.summary: Compute and display a transparent design summary (weight, thrust, thrust-to-weight, flight time range) that updates as the design changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary panel shows the required metrics.
Assumptions/inputs used for calculations are visible (e.g., per-motor thrust estimate, battery capacity, efficiency factor).
Metrics update when relevant component parameters change.

file_operations.description: Define user-visible default assumptions/constants used in calculations (e.g., estimated thrust per motor vs prop size/KV band, efficiency factor, average current draw heuristic).
Implement pure functions that take the current design configuration and assumptions and return estimated all-up weight, total thrust, thrust-to-weight ratio, and flight time range.

Render a summary panel with the required metrics plus a clearly labeled section listing the assumptions/inputs used. Use shadcn-ui Card/Table components for readable layout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the DesignSummaryPanel into the workspace and ensure it updates immediately when design parameters change.

requirements.summary: Add a “My Designs” panel that uses React Query to save, list, load, rename, and delete designs via the backend actor, with loading/error states.
acceptanceCriteria: User can save the current design with a name.
Saved designs list shows at least name and last updated time.
User can load a saved design and see the editor + 3D preview update accordingly.
User can rename and delete a saved design.
UI shows loading and error states for all backend actions.

file_operations.description: Implement serialization/deserialization between in-memory design config and the backend's designData string payload; include versioning metadata to support future changes.

Create React Query hooks for listDesigns, getDesign, saveDesign, and deleteDesign using frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts; include consistent error handling for authorization failures and network errors.

Implement the My Designs panel UI: save current design with a name, list designs with last updated time, load into editor, rename (implemented as save under new name + delete old name if needed), and delete. Use shadcn-ui Dialog/AlertDialog/Input/Button components and show loading/error states for all actions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire MyDesignsPanel into the workspace and connect load action to replace the current DesignStore state so both editor and 3D preview update.
Use auth gating to disable save/load/list/rename/delete when signed out and provide a helpful in-panel message instructing the user to sign in.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent, tool-like visual theme across landing and workspace (non-blue/purple primary palette).
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page and workspace share a consistent visual system (type scale, spacing, buttons, panels).
Theme avoids default/unstyled appearance; UI looks intentional and tool-like.
Theme does not rely on blue/purple as the primary palette.

file_operations.description: Create the global stylesheet imported by frontend/src/main.tsx and define the design tokens (CSS variables) to establish a cohesive theme (e.g., dark neutral workspace + warm accent such as orange/amber/green) avoiding blue/purple as primary.
Create a reusable app shell layout component that standardizes spacing, panels, and typography across pages; use it in App routing layout.
Wrap routed pages in the AppShell and ensure the theme (including dark mode class if used) is applied consistently across landing and workspace.
Align landing page layout and components to the shared theme (typography scale, spacing rhythm, buttons/panels) so it matches the workspace look and feel.

Ensure workspace panels (editor, 3D preview, summary, My Designs) use consistent themed containers and spacing (compose shadcn-ui Card/Separator/etc., without modifying frontend/src/components/ui). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add generated hero and logo images as static frontend assets and display them on the landing page and in the app header.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images are placed under frontend/public/assets/generated and referenced from the UI.
Landing page includes the hero image.
App includes a logo mark in the header or equivalent app chrome.

file_operations.description: Add the generated hero image asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/drone-hero.dim_1600x900.png for static usage (no backend involvement).
Add the generated logo mark asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/drone-logo.dim_512x512.png for static usage (no backend involvement).
Reference and render the hero image from frontend/public/assets/generated/drone-hero.dim_1600x900.png in the landing page hero section.
Reference and render the logo mark from frontend/public/assets/generated/drone-logo.dim_512x512.png in the header/app chrome alongside navigation.
